summary: The reproduction starts a default FUXA server instance, submits an unauthenticated test script to /api/runscript that writes a proof file, and verifies code execution by reading the file contents back through a second unauthenticated /api/runscript request.
expected_security_impact: The exploit should execute attacker-supplied JavaScript in the server process and create a proof file that can then be read back, demonstrating arbitrary code execution.
actual_observed_result: The validated exploit request returned "ok", created /private/tmp/evohunt-rce-proof in the server runtime, and a second request returned the proof string "pwned".
verification_method: The run script starts the bundled server snapshot with default settings, sends one unauthenticated request that writes a proof file, then sends a second unauthenticated request that reads the proof file. Success requires the verifier response to contain the exact proof string rather than a generic error or startup artifact.
evidence_produced_by_run: The first POST /api/runscript exploit request returns "ok".
The second POST /api/runscript verifier request returns the proof string "pwned".
The proof file is created within the server runtime at /private/tmp/evohunt-rce-proof during validation.
limitations_or_blockers: The host-based run.sh reproduction expects node, npm, and curl to be available locally.
